hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (e.g., W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues (e.g., E192, K205), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (e.g., L, I, V) will improve steric fit in the SSTR2-unique TM6 pocket (e.g., L272, I273), thereby increasing selectivity margin over off-targets SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving good selectivity (Δmargin > 0), but structural and docking failures are limiting the number of viable candidates. Tightening clash constraints and increasing the candidate pool will help generate more robust designs.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures (e.g., pLDDT=0.0) suggest excessive clashes in the designed peptides. Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better backbone-geometry and side-chain packing.

The low pass rate (12.5%) indicates that the current search space is too narrow to find high-quality candidates. Increasing the number of candidates will improve the chances of generating structurally and docking-competent designs.
